Entrance to Konkani section of the Golden Heart Emporium, Margao, Goa

**Goan Konkani** refers to all the central dialects of the Konkani [macrolanguage](/wiki/Macrolanguage "Macrolanguage") except for those that fall under [Maharashtri Konkani](/wiki/Maharashtri_Konkani "Maharashtri Konkani"). These dialects are collectively assigned the language code `gom` under the [ISO 639-3](/wiki/ISO_639-3 "ISO 639-3") classification.

In common usage, Goan Konkani refers collectively only to those dialects of Konkani spoken primarily in the state of [Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa"), for eg. The Antruz, Bardeskari & Saxtti dialects. But in the broader linguistic context, Goan Konkani also includes dialects spoken outside the official boundaries of Goa, such as [Malvani Konkani](/wiki/Malvani_Konkani "Malvani Konkani"), Chitpavani Konkani, Karwari Konkani, [Mangalorean](/wiki/Mangalorean "Mangalorean") Konkani etc.

## Organisations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Organisations")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/61/India_Goa_TSKK.jpg/250px-India_Goa_TSKK.jpg)](/wiki/File:India_Goa_TSKK.jpg)

The campus of the [Thomas Stephens Konknni Kendr (TSKK)](/wiki/Thomas_Stephens_Konkkni_Kendr "Thomas Stephens Konkkni Kendr"), a research institute working on issues related to the Konkani language, located at Alto Porvorim, near [Panaji](/wiki/Panaji "Panaji") in Goa

There are organisations working for Konkani but, primarily, these were restricted to individual communities. The [All India Konkani Parishad](/wiki/All_India_Konkani_Parishad "All India Konkani Parishad") founded on 8 July 1939, provided a common ground for Konkani people from all regions.[[111]](#cite_note-118) A new organisation known as Vishwa Konkani Parishad, which aims to be an all-inclusive and pluralistic umbrella organisation for Konkanis around the world, was founded on 11 September 2005.

Mandd Sobhann is the premier organisation that is striving hard to preserve, promote, propagate, and enrich the Konkani language and culture. It all began with the experiment called 'Mandd Sobhann' – a search for a Konkani identity in Konkani music on 30 November 1986 at Mangalore. What began as a performance titled 'Mandd Sobhann', grew into a movement of revival and rejuvenation of Konkani culture; and solidified into an organization called Mandd Sobhann. Today, Mandd Sobhann boasts of all these 3 identities namely - a performance, a movement and an organization.
The Konkan Daiz Yatra, started in 1939 in Bombay (Mumbai), is the oldest Konkani organisation. The [Konkani Bhasha Mandal](/wiki/Konkani_Bhasha_Mandal "Konkani Bhasha Mandal") was born in Mumbai on 5 April 1942, during the Third Adhiveshan of [All India Konkani Parishad](/wiki/All_India_Konkani_Parishad "All India Konkani Parishad"). On 28 December 1984, [Goa Konkani Akademi](/wiki/Goa_Konkani_Akademi "Goa Konkani Akademi") (GKA) was founded by the government of Goa to promote Konkani language, literature, and culture.[[112]](#cite_note-119) The [Thomas Stephens Konknni Kendr (TSKK)](/wiki/Thomas_Stephens_Konkkni_Kendr "Thomas Stephens Konkkni Kendr") is a popular research institute based in the [Goan](/wiki/Goa "Goa") capital [Panaji](/wiki/Panaji "Panaji"). It works on issues related to the Konkani language, literature, culture, and education.[[113]](#cite_note-120) The [Dalgado Konkani Academy](/wiki/Dalgado_Konkani_Academy "Dalgado Konkani Academy") is a popular Konkani organisation based in Panaji.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/World_Konkani_Centre.jpg/250px-World_Konkani_Centre.jpg)](/wiki/File:World_Konkani_Centre.jpg)

World Konkani Centre, [Mangalore](/wiki/Mangalore "Mangalore")

The Konkani Triveni Kala Sangam is one more famed Konkani organisation in [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai"), which is engaged in the vocation of patronising Konkani language through the theatre movement. The [government of Karnataka](/wiki/Government_of_Karnataka "Government of Karnataka") established the Karnataka Konkani Sahitya Akademy on 20 April 1994.[[114]](#cite_note-121) The Konkani Ekvott is an umbrella organisation of the Konkani bodies in Goa.

The [First World Konkani Convention](/wiki/First_World_Konkani_Convention "First World Konkani Convention") was held in Mangalore in December 1995. The Konkani Language and Cultural Foundation came into being immediately after the World Konkani Convention in 1995.[[115]](#cite_note-122)

The [World Konkani Centre](/wiki/World_Konkani_Centre "World Konkani Centre") built on a three-acre plot called Konkani Gaon (Konkani Village) at Shakti Nagar, [Mangalore](/wiki/Mangalore "Mangalore") was inaugurated on 17 January 2009,[[116]](#cite_note-123) "to serve as a nodal agency for the preservation and overall development of Konkani language, art, and culture involving all the Konkani people the world over."

The North American Konkani Association (NAKA) serves to unite Konkanis across the United States and Canada. It serves as a parent organization for smaller Konkani associations in various states. Furthermore, the Konkani Young Adult Group serves as a platform under NAKA to allow young adults across America (18+) of Konkani descent to meet each other and celebrate their heritage. Every 2–4 years, a Konkani Sammelan, where Konkanis from across the continent attend, is held in a different city in the US. A Konkani Youth Convention is held yearly. Past locations have included [NYC](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") and [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta"); the upcoming youth convention is slated to be held in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), IL in June.

## Literature

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: Literature")]

Main article: [Konkani literature](/wiki/Konkani_literature "Konkani literature")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dc/Doutrina_Christam_%28book_of_Stephens%2C_1622%29.jpg/250px-Doutrina_Christam_%28book_of_Stephens%2C_1622%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Doutrina_Christam_(book_of_Stephens,_1622).jpg)

Cover of *Dovtrina Christam* by [Fr. Thomas Stephens](/wiki/Thomas_Stephens_(missionary) "Thomas Stephens (missionary)"), first published work in Konkani, and any Indian language

During the [Goa Inquisition](/wiki/Goa_Inquisition "Goa Inquisition") which commenced in 1560, all books found in the Konkani language were burnt, and it is possible that old Konkani literature was destroyed as a consequence.[[117]](#cite_note-onqui-124)

The earliest writer in the history of Konkani language known today is [Krishnadas Shama](/wiki/Krishnadas_Shama "Krishnadas Shama") from [Quelossim](/wiki/Quelossim "Quelossim") in Goa. He began writing 25 April 1526, and he authored *Ramayana*, *Mahabharata*, and *Krishnacharitrakatha* in [prose](/wiki/Prose "Prose") style. The manuscripts have not been found, although transliterations in Roman script are found in [Braga](/wiki/Braga "Braga") in Portugal. The script used by him for his work is not known.[[118]](#cite_note-sama-125)

The first known printed book in Konkani was written by an English [Jesuit](/wiki/Jesuit "Jesuit") priest, [Fr. Thomas Stephens](/wiki/Thomas_Stephens_(missionary) "Thomas Stephens (missionary)") in 1622, and entitled *[Doutrina Christam em Lingoa Bramana Canarim](/wiki/Doutrina_Christam_em_Lingoa_Bramana_Canarim "Doutrina Christam em Lingoa Bramana Canarim")* (Old Portuguese for: *Christian Doctrine in the Canarese Brahman Language*). The first book exclusively on Konkani grammar, *[Arte da Lingoa Canarim](/wiki/Arte_da_Lingoa_Canarim "Arte da Lingoa Canarim")*, was printed in 1640 by Father Stephens in Portuguese.[[42]](#cite_note-manohar-46)

## Media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Media")]

### Radio

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: Radio")]

[All India Radio](/wiki/All_India_Radio "All India Radio") started broadcasting Konkani news and other services. Radio Goa Pangim started a Konkani broadcast in 1945. AIR Mumbai and Dharwad later started Konkani broadcasts in the years 1952 and 1965 respectively. Portuguese Radio, Lisbon started services in 1955 for India, East Africa, and Portugal. Similarly [Trivandrum](/wiki/Trivandrum "Trivandrum"), [Alleppey](/wiki/Alleppey "Alleppey"), [Trichur](/wiki/Trichur "Trichur"), and [Calicut](/wiki/Kozhikode "Kozhikode") AIR centres started Konkani broadcasts.[[42]](#cite_note-manohar-46)

In Manglore and Udupi, many weekly news magazines are published in Konkani. *Rakno*, *Daize*, and a few others are very famous among the Christian community. Every Roman Catholic parish will publish three or four magazines in a year.

### Print

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Print")]

*Udentichem Sallok* was the first Konkani periodical published in 1888, from Poona, by [Eduardo Bruno de Souza](/w/index.php?title=Eduardo_Bruno_de_Souza&action=edit&redlink=1 "Eduardo Bruno de Souza (page does not exist)"). It started as a monthly and then as a fortnightly. It closed down in 1894.[[119]](#cite_note-126)

#### Dailies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Dailies")]

*Sanjechem Nokhetr* was started in 1907 by [B. F. Cabral](/wiki/B._F._Cabral "B. F. Cabral") in [Bombay](/wiki/Bombay "Bombay"), and is the first *Concanim* (or Konkani) newspaper. It contained detailed news of [Bombay](/wiki/Bombay "Bombay"), [British India](/wiki/British_India "British India"), as it was published from there. In 1982, *Novem Goem* was a daily edited by [Gurunath Kelekar](/wiki/Gurunath_Kelekar "Gurunath Kelekar"), F. M. Rebello and [Felicio Cardozo](/wiki/Felicio_Cardozo "Felicio Cardozo"). It was started due to people's initiative. In 1989, Fr. [Freddy J. da Costa](/w/index.php?title=Freddy_J._da_Costa&action=edit&redlink=1 "Freddy J. da Costa (page does not exist)"), began a Konkani daily *Goencho Avaz*. It became a monthly after one and a half year. Presently there is just a single Konkani daily newspaper, called *Bhaangar Bhuin*. For a long time, there was another Konkani daily, *[Sunaparant](/wiki/Sunaparant "Sunaparant")*, which was published in [Panjim](/wiki/Panjim "Panjim").

#### Weeklies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=42 "Edit section: Weeklies")]

*O Luzo-Concanim* was a Concanim (Konkani)- Portuguese bilingual weekly, begun in 1891, by Aleixo Caitano José Francisco. From 1892 to 1897, *A Luz, O Bombaim Esse, A Lua, "O Intra Jijent* and *O Opinião Nacional* were bilingual Concanim- Portuguese weeklies published. In 1907, *O Goano* was putblished from Bombay by Honorato Furtado and Francis Xavier Furtado. It was a trilingual weekly in Portuguese, Konkani and English.

The Society of the Missionaries of Saint Francis Xavier, publish the Konkani weekly (satollem) named *[Vauraddeancho Ixtt](/wiki/Vauraddeancho_Ixtt "Vauraddeancho Ixtt")*. from Pilar. It was started in 1933 by Fr. [Arsencio Fernandes](/w/index.php?title=Arsencio_Fernandes&action=edit&redlink=1 "Arsencio Fernandes (page does not exist)") and Fr. [Graciano Moraes](/w/index.php?title=Graciano_Moraes&action=edit&redlink=1 "Graciano Moraes (page does not exist)").

#### Fortnightly

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Fortnightly")]

There is a fortnightly published newspaper since 2007 called *Kodial Khaber*, edited by Venkatesh Baliga Mavinakurve and published by Baliga Publications, Mangalore.

#### Monthlies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=44 "Edit section: Monthlies")]

*[Katolik Sovostkai](/w/index.php?title=Katolik_Sovostkai&action=edit&redlink=1 "Katolik Sovostkai (page does not exist)")* was started in 1907 by Roldão Noronha. It later became a fortnightly before ceasing publication.

*[Dor Mhoineachi Rotti](/wiki/Dor_Mhoineachi_Rotti "Dor Mhoineachi Rotti")* is the oldest running Konkani periodical. It is dedicated to the spreading of the devotion to the [Sacred Heart of Jesus](/wiki/Sacred_Heart_of_Jesus "Sacred Heart of Jesus"), and was initially named Dor Muineachi Rotti Povitra Jesucha Calzachem Devoçãõ Vaddounchi. Note that the til (tilde mark) over ãõ in Devoçãõ is one single til. Fr. [Vincent Lobo](/wiki/Vincent_Lobo "Vincent Lobo"), from Sangolda in Goa, who was then curator at the St. Patrick's Church in Karachi, began it in 1915, to feed the spiritual thirst and hunger of the large number of Konkani speaking people there, on noticing the absence of Konkani spiritual literature. The name was changed subsequently to "Dor Muiniachi Rotti, Concanim Messenger of the Sacred Heart". On Fr. Vincent Lobo's death on 11 November 1922, Fr. [António Ludovico Pereira](/w/index.php?title=Ant%C3%B3nio_Ludovico_Pereira&action=edit&redlink=1 "António Ludovico Pereira (page does not exist)"), also from Sangolda, took over the responsibility. Dor Mhoineachi Rotti had an estimated readership of around 12,000 people then. After the death of Fr. António Ludovico Pereira on 26 July 1936, Fr. [Antanasio Moniz](/w/index.php?title=Antanasio_Moniz&action=edit&redlink=1 "Antanasio Moniz (page does not exist)"), from [Verna](/wiki/Verna,_Goa "Verna, Goa"), took over. On his death in 1953, Fr. [Elias D'Souza](/w/index.php?title=Elias_D%27Souza&action=edit&redlink=1 "Elias D'Souza (page does not exist)"), from [Bodiem](/w/index.php?title=Bodiem&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bodiem (page does not exist)"), [Tivim](/wiki/Tivim "Tivim") in [Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa") became the fourth editor of Dor Mhoineachi Rotti. After shifting to [Velha Goa](/wiki/Velha_Goa "Velha Goa") in [Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa") around 1964, Fr. [Moreno de Souza](/wiki/Moreno_de_Souza "Moreno de Souza") was editor for around 42 years. Presently the Dor Mhuineachi Rotti is owned by the [Jesuits](/wiki/Jesuits "Jesuits") in Goa, edited by Fr. [Vasco do Rego](/wiki/Vasco_do_Rego "Vasco do Rego"), S. J. and printed and published by Fr. Jose Silveira, S.J. on behalf of the Provincial Superior of the Jesuits in Goa. [Dor Mhoineachi Rotti](/wiki/Dor_Mhoineachi_Rotti "Dor Mhoineachi Rotti") will complete 100 years on 1 January 2015.

*Gulab* is a monthly from Goa. It was started by late Fr. Freddy J. da Costa in 1983, and was printed in colour, then uncommon.

Konkani periodicals published in Goa include *[Vauraddeancho Ixtt](/wiki/Vauraddeancho_Ixtt "Vauraddeancho Ixtt")* (Roman script, weekly), *Gulab* (Roman script, monthly), *Bimb* (Devanagari script, monthly), *Panchkadayi* (Kannada script, monthly) and *Poddbimb* (Roman script, monthly).
Konkani periodicals published in Mangalore include "Raknno" (Kannada script, weekly), "DIVO" (Kannada Script, weekly from Mumbai), "Kutmacho Sevak" (Kannada script, monthly), "Dirvem" (Kannada script, monthly),"Amcho Sandesh" (Kannada script, monthly) and "Kajulo" (Kannda script, children's magazine, monthly).
Konkani periodical published in Udupi include "Uzwad" (Kannada script, monthly) and Naman Ballok Jezu (Kannada script, monthly).
Ekvottavorvim Uzvadd (Devanagari Script, monthly) is published from Belgaum since 1998. Panchkadayi Konkani Monthly magazine from Manipal since 1967.

### Digital and audible

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=45 "Edit section: Digital and audible")]

The first complete literary website in Konkani started in 2001 using Kannada script was www.maaibhaas.com by Naveen Sequeira of Brahmavara. In 2003 www.daaiz.com started by Valley Quadros Ajekar from Kuwait, this literary portal was instrumental in creating a wider range of readers across the globe, apart from various columns, literary contests, through Ashawadi Prakashan, he published several books in Konkani, including the first e-book 'Sagorachea Vattecheo Zori' released by Gerry DMello Bendur in 2005 at Karkala.

www.poinnari.com is the first literary webportal in Konkani using three scripts (Kannada, Nagari and Romi), started in 2015, is also conducted the first National level literary contest in dual scripts in Konkani in 2017.

'Sagorachea Vattecheo Zori' is the first e-book in Konkani, a compilation of 100 poems digitally published by www.daaiz.com and digitally published in 2005 by Ashawadi Prakashan in Karkala.

'Kathadaaiz' is the first digital audio book digitally published in 2018 by www.poinnari.com. This audio book is also available in the YouTube channel of Ashawari Prakashan.

'Pattim Gamvak' is the first e-Novel written in Kannada script Konkani in 2002 by Valley Quadros Ajekar from Kuwait, published in www.maaibhaas.com in 2002–3.

'Veez' is the first digital weekly in Konkani, started in 2018 by Austine D'Souza Prabhu in Chicago, USA. Veez is the only magazine publishing Konkani in 4 scripts; Kannada, Nagari, Romi and Malayalam.

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Konkani_language&action=edit&section=49 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

Many Konkani songs of the Goan fisher-folk appear recurrently in a number of [Hindi films](/wiki/Hindi_films "Hindi films"). Many Hindi movies feature characters with a Goan Catholic accent. A famous song from the 1957 movie *[Aasha](/wiki/Aasha_(1957_film) "Aasha (1957 film)")*, contains the Konkani words "mhaka naka" and became extremely popular. Children were chanting "[Eeny, meeny, miny, moe](/wiki/Eeny,_meeny,_miny,_moe "Eeny, meeny, miny, moe")", which inspired [C Ramchandra](/wiki/C_Ramchandra "C Ramchandra") and his assistant John Gomes to create the first line of the song, "Eena Meena Deeka, De Dai Damanika". Gomes, who was a [Goan](/wiki/Goan "Goan"), added the words "mhaka naka" (Konkani for "I don't want"). They kept on adding more nonsense rhymes until they ended with "Rum pum po!".[[121]](#cite_note-bolly-128)[[122]](#cite_note-ashwin_story-129)

An international ad campaign by [Nike](/wiki/Nike,_Inc. "Nike, Inc.") for the [2007 Cricket World Cup](/wiki/2007_Cricket_World_Cup "2007 Cricket World Cup") featured a Konkani song "Rav Patrao Rav" as the background theme. It was based on the tune of an older song "[Bebdo](/wiki/Bebdo "Bebdo")", composed by [Chris Perry](/wiki/Chris_Perry_(musician) "Chris Perry (musician)") and sung by [Lorna Cordeiro](/wiki/Lorna_Cordeiro "Lorna Cordeiro"). The new lyrics were written by Agnello Dias (who worked in the ad agency that made the ad), recomposed by Ram Sampat, and sung by Ella Castellino.

A Konkani cultural event called "Konkani Nirantari", was conducted by Mandd Sobhann, a [Mangalorean Christian](/wiki/Mangalorean_Christian "Mangalorean Christian") organisation founded in 1986 AD.[[123]](#cite_note-130)[[124]](#cite_note-131)[[125]](#cite_note-132) The event was held at [Mangalore](/wiki/Mangalore "Mangalore") on 26 and 27 January 2008, it entered the [Guinness Book of World Records](/wiki/Guinness_Book_of_World_Records "Guinness Book of World Records") for holding a 40-hour-long non-stop Konkani musical marathon; beating a [Brazilian](/wiki/Brazilian_people "Brazilian people") musical troupe, who had previously held the record of singing non-stop for 36 hours.[[126]](#cite_note-133)

1. **[^](#cite_ref-13)** [Devanagari](/wiki/Devanagari "Devanagari"): कोंकणी, [Romi](/wiki/Konkani_in_the_Roman_script "Konkani in the Roman script"): *Konknni*, [Kannada](/wiki/Kannada_script "Kannada script"): ಕೊಂಕಣಿ, [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam"): കൊങ്കണി, [Nastaliq](/wiki/Nastaliq "Nastaliq"): کونکنی;[[1]](#cite_note-MadhaviSardesai-1) [IAST](/wiki/IAST "IAST"): *Kōṅkṇī*, IPA: [[kõkɳi]](/wiki/Help:IPA "Help:IPA")

1. **[^](#cite_ref-7)** Roman script is not mandated as an official script by law. However, an ordinance passed by the government of Goa allows the use of Roman script for official communication. This ordinance has been put into effect by various ministries in varying degrees. For example, the 1996 Goa Panchayat Rules stipulate that the various forms used in the election process must be in both the Roman and Devanagari script. ["Panchayat Raj Act And Rules"](https://panchayatsgoa.gov.in/pdfdoc/Panchayat_Raj_Act_and_Rules.pdf?#zoom=100) (PDF). *panchayatsgoa.gov.in*. 1996. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20220610181357/https://panchayatsgoa.gov.in/pdfdoc/Panchayat_Raj_Act_and_Rules.pdf?#zoom=100) (PDF) from the original on 10 June 2022.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-8)** The use of Kannada script is not mandated by any law or ordinance. However, in the state of Karnataka, Konkani is used in the Kannada script instead of the Devanagari script.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-12)** Konkani is a name given to a group of several cognate dialects spoken along the narrow strip of land called [Konkan](/wiki/Konkan "Konkan"), on the western coast of India. Geographically, Konkan is defined roughly as the area between the [Daman Ganga River](/wiki/Daman_Ganga_River "Daman Ganga River") to the north and the [Kali River](/wiki/Kali_River_(Karnataka) "Kali River (Karnataka)") to the south; the north–south length is about 650 km and the east–west breadth is about 50 km. The dialect spoken in Goa, coastal Karnataka and in some parts of Northern Kerala has distinct features and is rightly identified as a separate language called Konkani.[[9]](#cite_note-11)
4. **[^](#cite_ref-75)** [Chavundaraya](/wiki/Chavundaraya "Chavundaraya") was the military chief of the Ganga dynasty-era King Gangaraya. This inscription on the Bahubali statue draws attention to a Basadi (Jain Temple) initially built by him and then modified by Gangaraya in the 12th century AD. Ref: S. Settar in Adiga (2006), p256
5. **[^](#cite_ref-76)** The above inscription has been quite controversial, and is touted as old-Marathi. But the distinctive instrumental *viyalem* ending of the verb is the hallmark of the Konkani language, and the verb *sutatale* or *sutatalap* is not prevalent in Marathi. So linguists and historians such as S.B. Kulkarni of Nagpur University, V.P. Chavan (former vice-president of the Anthropological Society of Mumbai), and others have thus concluded that it is Konkani.
6. **[^](#cite_ref-102)** /f/ was introduced into Konkani phonology via. Portuguese and largely replaces /pʰ/ in Goan and Mangalorean Christian dialects, however /pʰ/ is still retained in formal speech.
